mechanism_narrative: A contemporaneous month-over-month increase in the effective federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, causing an immediate negative revaluation of the S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it triggers a non-linear repricing of recession risk as corporate earnings expectations are slashed, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, the S&P 500 posts positive 60-day forward returns in greater than 45% of cases, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates combined with rising nonfarm payrolls sustain restrictive financial conditions for longer than markets price, jointly compressing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and PAYEMS fails to produce a statistically negative coefficient (t-stat < 2.0) in predicting 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling regression, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales predict positive S&P 500 returns only when the unemployment rate is below 5.0%, because job security is required to convert spending into sustainable corporate earnings rather than drawdown of savings.
failure_condition: During periods with UNRATE below 5.0%, month-over-month increases in RSAFS are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the long-only signal IR falls below 0.3 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing profitability and capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, month-over-month increases in the consumer price index force markets to price additional tightening, raising the equity risk premium and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal's Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth coinciding with contracting industrial production creates a demand-supply imbalance that raises input cost pressures and margin contraction fears, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising RSAFS and falling INDPRO fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a hit rate above 55%, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll growth drives positive S&P 500 returns only when consumer price inflation is stable, because elevated inflation causes the Federal Reserve to offset employment gains with tighter policy.
regime_hint: low and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL monthly change below 0.4)
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL monthly changes are below 0.4 index points, increases in PAYEMS are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year window.
